evals.name: nextjs-update-profile
evals.prompt: Compile a flow map for this Next.js project. Treat the current working directory as the target repo. Run the full compile-and-self-check loop and write everything under `.flow-map/`.
evals.expected_output: A `.flow-map/` directory containing AGENTS.md, APP.md, glossary.md, plus skills/, flows/, and endpoints/ subdirectories. The compiler should identify exactly one user-facing flow (profile editing on /profile), one business-domain skill that covers the profile read+write (e.g. `account` / `users` / `profile`), and exactly one endpoint backing the profile write.
expectations: Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter has counts.skills=1, counts.flows=1, counts.endpoints=1.
Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter sets stack.framework to `nextjs` (or a clear next.js identifier) and stack.language to `ts`.

Output `.flow-map/skills/` contains exactly one file. Its frontmatter `id` is a business-domain name (e.g. `account` / `users` / `profile`), and `suggested_endpoints[]` contains exactly one entry whose `endpoint` value matches an endpoint id under `endpoints/`.

Output `.flow-map/flows/` contains exactly one file describing the profile-edit user journey, with `entry` pointing at `app/profile/page.tsx` and frontmatter `skills_used[]` referencing the single skill (no role field).
Output `.flow-map/endpoints/` contains exactly one file. Its frontmatter has `method: PUT|POST|PATCH`, `path` contains `/users/`, and `used_by_skills[]` lists the single skill's id.
No `capabilities/` directory exists.
No `tools-proposed.json` file exists at the root of `.flow-map/`.
Output `.flow-map/glossary.md` is the thin pivot table with columns: Skill, User phrases, Suggested endpoints, Flows. One row.

No flow body file in `.flow-map/flows/` and no skill body file in `.flow-map/skills/` contains an HTTP verb token (`GET|POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE`), `fetch(`, `axios.`, or any `/api/` URL path. Those appear only in `endpoints/`.

evals.name: react-update-profile
evals.prompt: Compile a flow map for this Vite + React + react-router-dom project. Treat the current working directory as the target repo. Run the full compile-and-self-check loop and write everything under `.flow-map/`.
evals.expected_output: A `.flow-map/` directory whose Skills/Flows/Endpoints tables describe a single user journey: the user edits their profile on a `/profile` route. One business-domain skill covering the profile read+write, one endpoint for the write.
expectations: Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter has counts.skills=1, counts.flows=1, counts.endpoints=1.
Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter sets stack.framework to `react`, stack.router to `react-router-dom` (or equivalent), stack.language to `ts`.

Output `.flow-map/skills/` contains exactly one file. Its frontmatter `id` is a business-domain name (e.g. `account` / `users` / `profile`), and `suggested_endpoints[]` contains exactly one entry whose `endpoint` value matches an endpoint id under `endpoints/`.

Output `.flow-map/flows/` contains exactly one file with `entry` pointing at `src/pages/Profile.tsx`, and frontmatter `skills_used[]` referencing the single skill via `skill_ref: ../skills/<id>.md` and no `role:` key.

Output `.flow-map/endpoints/` contains exactly one file. Its frontmatter has `method: PUT|POST|PATCH`, `path` contains `/users/`, `source` resolves to `src/api/users.ts`, and `used_by_skills[]` lists the single skill.
No `capabilities/` directory exists.
No `tools-proposed.json` file exists at the root of `.flow-map/`.
Output `.flow-map/glossary.md` is the thin pivot table with columns: Skill, User phrases, Suggested endpoints, Flows. One row.
Round-trip: the skill lists the endpoint in `suggested_endpoints[].endpoint`, and the endpoint lists the skill in `used_by_skills[]`.
No flow body or skill body contains an HTTP verb token, `fetch(`, `axios.`, or any `/api/` URL path.

evals.name: sveltekit-view-home
evals.prompt: Compile a flow map for this SvelteKit project. Treat the current working directory as the target repo. Run the full compile-and-self-check loop and write everything under `.flow-map/`.
evals.expected_output: A `.flow-map/` directory describing a single read-only flow: the home route loads and reads ping records from the backend. One business-domain skill (e.g. `health` / `ping`), one endpoint.
expectations: Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter has counts.skills=1, counts.flows=1, counts.endpoints=1.
Output `.flow-map/AGENTS.md` frontmatter sets stack.framework to `sveltekit` and stack.router to `filesystem` (or equivalent).
Output `.flow-map/skills/` contains exactly one file whose `suggested_endpoints[]` has exactly one entry with `role: read`.
Output `.flow-map/flows/` contains exactly one file with `entry` pointing at `src/routes/+page.svelte` and `skills_used[]` referencing the single skill (no role field).
Output `.flow-map/endpoints/` contains exactly one file. Its frontmatter has `method: GET`, `path: /api/ping`, `auth: none`, and `used_by_skills[]` lists the single skill.
The endpoint `auth` field is one of the schema-allowed values `bearer|cookie|none` (NOT `unknown` or any other free-form string).
No `capabilities/` directory exists.
No `tools-proposed.json` file exists at the root of `.flow-map/`.
Output `.flow-map/glossary.md` is the thin pivot table only — no `## Intent anchors` section.
No flow body or skill body contains `fetch(`, an HTTP verb token, or `/api/` URL path.
